source.transliteration: IVTFF/EVA (ZL file as downloaded by scripts/build_pages.py)

starter_note: Optional generic starter/activator (e.g., brewing yeast, sourdough starter liquid, or a safe culture), only if the `p` marker is present.
safe_complex_herbal_blend: gentle spices (e.g., 1 g cinnamon + 1 g clove) or a commercial herbal tea blend

page_summary_procedural.disclaimer: IMPORTANT: this is NOT a real or validated translation of the Voynich Manuscript. It is a speculative/procedural model that interprets EVA using a user-defined grammar to generate experimental recipes using safe, known edible substitutes.
page_summary_procedural.expected_result: A mild herbal preparation (infusion/extract) whose intensity depends on the detected level markers; a live-culture step is optional and only suggested when `p` appears.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure risk: avoid sealed bottling if you used a starter culture or see active gas production.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental procedural model.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, reduce sugar by 25–50% or omit it entirely (many processes may be non-fermentative).
If you did not intend any live-culture step, do not add a starter even if `p` appears; keep the result refrigerated as an infusion/extract.

line_recipes.eva_line: pchroiin otchor chpchol chpchey s pchocty

recipe.disclaimer: IMPORTANT: this is NOT a real or validated translation of the Voynich Manuscript. It is a speculative/procedural model that interprets EVA using a user-defined grammar to generate experimental recipes using safe, known edible substitutes.

parsing.interpretation: add main plant (safe substitute) → mix / transfer → add starter / activate → duration level 2 → state: cooling/rest → medium phase
apply heat/cooking → add main plant (safe substitute) → mix / transfer
add main plant (safe substitute) → mix / transfer → add starter / activate
add main plant (safe substitute) → add starter / activate → duration level 1 → state: active extraction
[unparsed]
apply heat/cooking → add main plant (safe substitute) → mix / transfer → add starter / activate

ingredients.starter_note: Optional generic starter/activator (e.g., brewing yeast, sourdough starter liquid, or a safe culture), only if the `p` marker is present.

process: Sanitize the vessel and utensils (good practice for any wet process).
Base: combine 0.5 L water with 25 g sugar or honey (or reduce/omit if you want a non-sweet infusion).
Apply gentle heat: simmer 10–15 min (if present in markers), then cool to <30°C before any starter is added.
Add main plant: chamomile (safe default substitute) (~10 g dried).
Add secondary herb: mint (~2 g dried).
Add starter: 1 g (generic activation step; exact culture depends on your chosen domain interpretation).
Hold for the indicated phase: 3–5 days (guided by dy/iin/aiin cues).
Strain/filter if needed, then rest cold 12–24 h for clarity (optional).
Store refrigerated. If the process becomes carbonated/pressurized, vent carefully and avoid sealed bottling unless you know it is stable.
recipe.expected_result: A mild herbal preparation (infusion/extract) whose intensity depends on the detected level markers; a live-culture step is optional and only suggested when `p` appears.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure risk: avoid sealed bottling if you used a starter culture or see active gas production.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental procedural model.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, reduce sugar by 25–50% or omit it entirely (many processes may be non-fermentative).
If you did not intend any live-culture step, do not add a starter even if `p` appears; keep the result refrigerated as an infusion/extract.

plant_interpretation.note: Heuristic classification based on the IVTFF 'Plant ID' string (not the drawing). Does not imply real identification of the manuscript plant.

Base: combine 0.5 L water with 25 g sugar or honey (or reduce/omit if you want a non-sweet infusion).
Apply gentle heat: simmer 10–15 min (if present in markers), then cool to <30°C before any starter is added.
Add main plant: chamomile (safe default substitute) (~5 g dried).
Add secondary herb: mint (~1 g dried).
Hold for the indicated phase: 7–14 days (guided by dy/iin/aiin cues).
Strain/filter if needed, then rest cold 12–24 h for clarity (optional).
Store refrigerated. If the process becomes carbonated/pressurized, vent carefully and avoid sealed bottling unless you know it is stable.
recipe.expected_result: A mild herbal preparation (infusion/extract) whose intensity depends on the detected level markers; a live-culture step is optional and only suggested when `p` appears.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure risk: avoid sealed bottling if you used a starter culture or see active gas production.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental procedural model.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, reduce sugar by 25–50% or omit it entirely (many processes may be non-fermentative).
If you did not intend any live-culture step, do not add a starter even if `p` appears; keep the result refrigerated as an infusion/extract.

process: Sanitize the vessel and utensils (good practice for any wet process).
Base: combine 0.5 L water with 25 g sugar or honey (or reduce/omit if you want a non-sweet infusion).
Infusion/extraction: use hot (not boiling) water, then let it cool before any starter is added.
Add main plant: chamomile (safe default substitute) (~2 g dried).
Add secondary herb: mint (~1 g dried).
Hold for the indicated phase: 1–3 days (guided by dy/iin/aiin cues).
Strain/filter if needed, then rest cold 12–24 h for clarity (optional).
Store refrigerated. If the process becomes carbonated/pressurized, vent carefully and avoid sealed bottling unless you know it is stable.

Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
